item.title: Open BSD and Zen
item.description: OpenBSD and Zen I’ve been using OpenBSD for a long time. I’ve been sitting Zen for about two years, in the tradition of Seungsahn, with Ryōkan as a guide. Nobody asked me to connect these two things. But they kept connecting themselves. This is me trying to say why. Less is not a compromise OpenBSD ships without a lot of things other systems include by default. That’s a choice. Every piece of code that isn’t there is a piece of code that can’t have a vulnerability. Every feature you don’t ship is a surface you don’t have to defend.
